intent: We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS AI coding agent with a two-process architecture (Swift shell + Python backend) that uses dual-model consensus AI (Claude + GPT-4o) to autonomously decompose build intents into ordered PRDs and PRs, generate implementation and test code, run self-correction/lint/fix loops, execute CI, and gate on operator approval. The system encompasses 16 TRD-specified subsystems: macOS application shell with Touch ID/Keychain/XPC (TRD-1), consensus engine with parallel generation and Claude arbitration (TRD-2), confidence-gated build pipeline with pr_type routing (TRD-3), multi-agent coordination via GitHub-stored ledger (TRD-4), GitHub integration with rate limiting and CI workflow management (TRD-5), holistic 5-lens code review (TRD-6), adaptive TRD development workflow with FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes (TRD-7), full SwiftUI design system with health dashboard and remediation stream (TRD-8), self-hosted Mac CI runner infrastructure (TRD-9), FAISS-backed document store with injection defense (TRD-10), comprehensive security threat model (TRD-11), backend startup/shutdown sequence with version handshake (TRD-12), three-layer recovery and state management with build memory and self-improving build rules (TRD-13), seven-fix CI false-positive pipeline (TRD-14), operational runbook (TRD-15), FM-taxonomy testing with 35 regression tests (TRD-16), and self-healing autonomous observability and remediation loop (TRD-17). Five additional subsystems identified as gaps (TRD-18 Crafted Connect SDK, Figma connector, deployment infrastructure, email notification, and webhook receiver) will be resolved during PRD decomposition using best practices as confirmed by operator.

Clarifications:
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices. This needs to be a configurable inout in the app
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - on by default, fall back if not discovered
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.
  - TRD-18 was combined with TRD-17

Operator on 'Is this build intent for the full system (all 17 TRDs from s':
build intent for the full system

Operator on 'The repo already contains a v38.153.0 codebase with 30+ Pyth':
generate a fresh implementation

Operator on 'TRD-17 (Self-Healing) and TRD-8 v3.0 (Health Dashboard) both':
TRD-18 was combined with TRD-17

Operator on 'The Figma design pipeline (TRD-8 §14, §24) references Figma ':
yes. Resolve as part of the PRD process using best practices.

prd_plan.title: Foundation, Monorepo Bootstrap, Versioning, Configuration, and Shared Contracts
macOS Native Shell, App Lifecycle, Touch ID/Keychain Auth, Settings, and Backend Process Host
Authenticated XPC/Unix Socket Channel, Wire Protocol, Handshake, and Command Routing
Backend Runtime Startup, Ready Handshake, Degraded States, and Graceful Shutdown
Document Store, FAISS Retrieval, Embeddings, Injection Defense, and Product Knowledge Loading
Consensus Engine, Dual-Model Provider Adapters, Prompt Systems, Arbitration, and Native Output Enforcement
GitHub Integration, Repository Bootstrap, PR Lifecycle, Rate Limiting, and CI Workflow Management
Webhook Receiver, Event Ingestion, and Polling Fallback
Confidence-Gated Build Pipeline, PRD/PR Planning, pr_type Routing, and Execution Orchestration
Repo Context Fetch, Self-Correction Loop, Lint Gate, Failure Classification, and Local Fix Loop
Multi-Agent Coordination, Build Ledger, Journals, Conflict Detection, and Live Sync
Recovery, Thread State, Build Memory, Build Rules, Context Management, and Resume Semantics
Holistic Five-Lens Code Review Workflow and Fix PR Generation
Adaptive TRD Development Workflow with FOUNDER, ENGINEER, and CONSULTANT Modes
SwiftUI Design System, Build Stream, Health Dashboard, Remediation UX, and Figma Pipeline
Security Threat Model Enforcement, Path Safety, Audit Logging, and Operational Runbook
Mac CI Runner Infrastructure, Swift Validation Workflow, Signing, Notarization, and Release Artifacts
Testing Framework, FM-Taxonomy Regression Suite, and End-to-End Validation
Observability, Self-Healing Loop, Autonomous Remediation, Crafted Connect SDK, and Embedded Issue Reporting

1.title: Scaffold repository directory layout and root files
1.summary: Create the canonical directory tree (forge-docs/, forge-standards/, src/, src/shared/, Crafted/, Crafted/Shared/, Crafted/Shared/Config/, CraftedTests/, tests/, tests/shared/, tests/shared/config/, tests/integration/, scripts/, config/, docs/, .github/workflows/) with __init__.py and .gitkeep sentinel files. Add root .gitignore, .editorconfig, LICENSE placeholder, and top-level README.md describing the repository layout and module ownership boundaries.

2.title: Add architecture standards and conventions documents
2.summary: Create the canonical standards documents in forge-standards/: ARCHITECTURE.md (two-process architecture overview, module dependency graph, data flow), INTERFACES.md (XPC protocol surface, config loading API, shared schema contracts), CONVENTIONS.md (naming rules, import ordering, docstring format, commit message format, branch naming), DECISIONS.md (ADR log — initial entries for monorepo choice, Pydantic v2, StrEnum, TOML config, no secrets in source), build_rules.md (initial self-improving build rules template).

3.title: Add Python project metadata, dependency manifests, and toolchain config
3.summary: Create the authoritative VERSION file at the repo root (initial value 0.1.0). Add scripts/sync_version.py that reads VERSION and patches pyproject.toml version field and Crafted Info.plist CFBundleShortVersionString. The script is idempotent and exits non-zero on mismatch when run in --check mode for CI gating. Add scripts/bump_version.py for semver increment operations.

4.title: Add Swift package manifest and Xcode project skeleton
4.summary: Create pyproject.toml with project metadata (name, version read from VERSION, Python >=3.12), dependency groups (runtime, dev, test), and inline tool configuration for ruff (select rules, line-length 120, src paths), mypy (strict mode, per-module overrides for src/shared), and pytest (testpaths, markers, minimum coverage threshold). Add py.typed marker in src/shared/.

5.title: Add Python package skeleton, version module, and shared domain enums
5.summary: Create scripts/bootstrap.sh that checks Python version, creates a venv, installs dependencies from pyproject.toml in editable mode, verifies ruff/mypy/pytest are available, and runs version sync in --check mode. Add a top-level Makefile with targets: bootstrap, lint, typecheck, test, fmt, version-check, clean. The bootstrap script prints a summary table of tool versions on success.

6.title: Define shared error taxonomy and exception hierarchy
6.summary: Implement the shared Python enums in src/shared/enums.py consumed by every subsystem. Includes BuildStage (decompose, generate, review, test, lint_fix, ci, gate), PRType (implementation, documentation, test), ConfidenceLevel (low, medium, high with numeric thresholds), AgentMode (FOUNDER, ENGINEER, CONSULTANT), HealthStatus (healthy, degraded, critical), and ProviderName (claude, gpt4o). All enums use StrEnum for JSON serialization compatibility. Add src/shared/constants.py for shared magic values and thresholds.

7.title: Define shared data schemas — PRD, PR, BuildIntent, CIResult, HealthStatus
7.summary: Implement src/shared/errors.py with a hierarchical exception taxonomy: ForgeBaseError as root, then ConfigurationError, ProviderError (with sub-types RateLimitError, AuthenticationError, ProviderTimeoutError), ConsensusError, PipelineError, GitHubAPIError, CIError, RecoveryError, SecurityError, VersionMismatchError. Each error carries a machine-readable error_code string and optional context dict. Add src/shared/error_codes.py with the canonical FORGE-{SUBSYSTEM}-{NNN} code registry ensuring uniqueness.

8.title: Define XPC message protocol types and operator I/O settings (Python side)
8.summary: Implement src/shared/schemas.py with Pydantic v2 BaseModel classes for cross-subsystem contracts: PRDReference (id, title, status), PRReference (pr_num, title, pr_type, depends_on, status), BuildIntent (description, scope, metadata), ConsensusResult (provider_outputs list, chosen_output, confidence, rationale), ReviewResult (lens, passed, findings list, severity), PipelineState (build_id, current_stage, pr_refs, started_at, error), VersionInfo (version, git_sha, python_version). All models use strict mode and forbid extra fields.

9.title: Tests for shared contracts — serialization round-trips and stability
9.summary: Create Swift-side shared enums in Crafted/Shared/ that mirror the Python enums for XPC boundary type safety. Includes Crafted/Shared/SharedEnums.swift (BuildStage, PRType, ConfidenceLevel, AgentMode, HealthStatus, ProviderName — all Codable, String-backed) and Crafted/Shared/SharedConstants.swift with threshold values matching Python constants.

10.title: Implement typed configuration models with validation and secret exclusion
10.summary: Create Crafted/Shared/SharedErrors.swift with ForgeError enum with associated values matching Python error codes and stable error_code mapping. Create Crafted/Shared/SharedSchemas.swift with Codable structs for VersionInfo, PipelineState, HealthReport, PRReference used in XPC messages. Includes Crafted/Shared/ErrorCodes.swift for the canonical code registry.

11.title: Implement configuration loader with layered resolution and environment override
11.summary: Create tests that validate the Python and Swift shared enums remain in sync. tests/shared/test_contract_parity.py parses Swift source files for enum case names and compares against Python StrEnum members, failing if any enum has a case present on one side but not the other. Also validates error code registries match. Add tests/fixtures/shared_contract_sample.json as a canonical serialization fixture and forge-standards/SHARED_CONTRACTS.md documenting the parity contract.

12.title: Add source-controlled configuration templates and environment examples
12.summary: Create the typed configuration system in src/shared/config/. Implements base config infrastructure (src/shared/config/base.py) and core models in src/shared/config/models.py: AIProviderConfig, GitHubConfig, CIConfig, ObservabilityConfig, SelfHealingConfig, ConsensusConfig, PipelineConfig, DocumentStoreConfig, RecoveryConfig, FeatureTogglesConfig, and root ForgeConfig that composes all sub-configs. Secret fields are typed as SecretStr and never embedded in source. All models validate on construction with Pydantic.

13.title: Tests for configuration precedence, secrets policy, and I/O behavior
13.summary: Build the config loading pipeline in src/shared/config/loader.py that reads from TOML files with environment variable overlay (FORGE_ prefix). Add src/shared/config/redaction.py for safe logging of config values with secret fields masked. Layering order: defaults.toml → config/local.toml → env vars. Loader validates required fields and exits with clear error messages on missing secrets.

14.title: Define Swift shared domain enums and error taxonomy mirroring Python
14.summary: Create config/defaults.toml with all non-secret default values organized by section matching ForgeConfig sub-models. Create config/.env.example listing every FORGE_ environment variable with placeholder descriptions, clearly marking which are required secrets. Create config/README.md documenting the config layering order, operator-configurable inputs, and how to add new settings.

15.title: Define Swift XPC contract models and I/O settings mirroring Python
15.summary: Implement src/shared/config/feature_toggles.py providing a FeatureToggleManager that reads the feature_toggles section of ForgeConfig, exposes is_enabled(toggle_name) -> bool, supports runtime reload from config without restart, emits a toggle_changed event for observers, and defaults all toggles to their config/defaults.toml values. Toggles include: dual_model_consensus (on by default, falls back to single provider if second not configured), self_healing, touch_id_auth, ci_auto_trigger, figma_connector.

16.title: Add Swift configuration models and decoding support
16.summary: Create Swift-side Codable configuration models in Crafted/Shared/Config/ that mirror the Python config models for XPC boundary type safety: RuntimeConfig, BuildConfig, AIConfig, ServiceConfig (GitHub/CI/Observability), and a root AppConfig composing them. These are used for configuration handshake between processes.

17.title: Add cross-language contract fixture tests for Swift-Python parity
17.summary: Create scripts/check_no_secrets.py that scans all source-controlled files for patterns matching API keys, tokens, passwords, and other secret material. Integrates with the config system to verify SecretStr fields never appear in TOML defaults. Exits non-zero if any potential secret is detected. Designed for CI gating.

18.title: Implement version synchronization tooling for Python and Swift metadata
18.summary: Focused regression tests validating: secret fields are never serialized to JSON/TOML without redaction, environment override of SecretStr fields works correctly, config loader rejects files containing plaintext secrets in non-secret fields, redaction covers all SecretStr paths in ForgeConfig, and error_codes across the error taxonomy are globally unique.

19.title: Add developer bootstrap script and local validation commands
19.summary: Create .github/workflows/crafted-ci.yml that runs on push/PR: checkout, setup Python 3.12, install dependencies, ruff check + format --check, mypy src/, scripts/sync_version.py --check, scripts/check_no_secrets.py, pytest with coverage report, upload coverage artifact. Create .github/workflows/crafted-ci-macos.yml for macOS Swift builds (xcodebuild test). Uses matrix strategy for ubuntu-latest.

20.title: Add Makefile with standard dev commands and pre-commit hooks
20.summary: End-to-end integration tests validating the complete foundation layer works together: (1) ForgeConfig loads from defaults.toml with env var overlays and rejects missing required secrets, (2) all shared schemas round-trip through JSON serialization (Pydantic → JSON → Pydantic), (3) VersionInfo constructed from VERSION file matches pyproject.toml, (4) feature toggles respect config-driven defaults and env overrides, (5) error taxonomy error_codes are unique across all error classes, (6) bootstrap script exit codes are correct.

21.title: Add CI workflow skeleton for lint, type-check, test, and Swift build
21.summary: Create comprehensive developer documentation: docs/DEVELOPMENT.md (local setup instructions referencing bootstrap.sh, Makefile targets, IDE recommendations), docs/CONFIGURATION.md (full config surface reference, layering rules, how to add new settings, secret handling policy), docs/VERSIONING.md (version authority, sync script usage, release tagging workflow). Update root README.md with quickstart pointing to these docs.

22.title: Add developer setup guide, configuration policy, and operator config reference
22.summary: Create README.md with project overview and quickstart. Create docs/DEVELOPER_SETUP.md with step-by-step local setup instructions referencing bootstrap.sh and Makefile targets. Create docs/CONFIGURATION_POLICY.md documenting the layered config strategy and secret handling rules. Create forge-standards/OPERATOR_CONFIG_GUIDE.md documenting every configurable input/output, its default, override mechanism, and which subsystem it affects. Create docs/VERSIONING.md explaining the single-source VERSION strategy.
